nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Soddington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire and Lincolnshire): habitational name from any of various places, for example in Cheshire, Derbyshire, Herefordshire, and Nottinghamshire, named Coddington, from the Old English personal name Cot(t)a + -ing- denoting association + tūn ‘settlement’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Ralph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

variants.name: Ralf
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When William Ralph de Soddington was born in 1250, in Warwickshire, England, his father, William de Soddington, was 31 and his mother, Joan d'Abenhale, was 25. He died about 1301, in Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 52.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Ralph de Soddington est né en 1250, à Warwickshire, Angleterre, son père, William de Soddington, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Joan d'Abenhale, avait 25 ans. Il est décédé environ 1301, à Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 52 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Ralph de Soddington 1250 in Warwickshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de Soddington 31 und seine Mutter Joan d'Abenhale 25. Er starb um 1301 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Ralph de Soddington è nato il nel 1250 nel Warwickshire, England, il suo padre, William de Soddington, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Joan d'Abenhale, aveva 25 anni. Morì circa 1301, nel Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 52 anni.
